description: Remove AUTO/SEMI/MANUAL badges from checklist and hide rules where trigger conditions weren't met (MFE-based relevance filtering)

userStories.title: Add isRuleRelevant Function
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a utility function that determines if a rule was relevant to a specific trade based on MFE, so that irrelevant rules can be filtered out.
acceptanceCriteria: Add isRuleRelevant(condition: AutoCondition, mfeR: number | null): boolean to src/lib/strategy/evaluation.ts
Returns true for always-relevant rules: maxRiskPerTrade, minRRRatio, dailyLossLimit, maxConcurrentPositions
Returns mfeR >= triggerR for breakevenTrigger
Returns mfeR >= targetR for scaleOutAtR
Returns mfeR >= triggerR for trailingStopTrigger
Returns true for unknown condition types (safe default)
Typecheck passes (bun run check)
Build passes (bun run build)
userStories.notes: Added isRuleRelevant function with exhaustive switch statement covering all AutoCondition types

userStories.title: Update getTradeRuleChecks to Return Relevance Data
userStories.description: As a frontend, I want the getTradeRuleChecks endpoint to include relevance information so that I can filter which rules to display.
acceptanceCriteria: Modify getTradeRuleChecks in src/server/api/routers/strategies.ts
Calculate MFE in R for the trade using existing calculateMfeInR()
For each rule with autoCondition, call isRuleRelevant()
Add relevantRuleIds: string[] to the response object
Typecheck passes (bun run check)
Build passes (bun run build)
userStories.notes: Added relevantRuleIds to getTradeRuleChecks response. Calculates MFE in R and uses isRuleRelevant() to determine which rules should be shown.

userStories.title: Integration Tests for Relevance Logic
userStories.description: As a developer, I want integration tests for the rule relevance logic to verify correct filtering behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Add tests to tests/integration/strategies.test.ts or create tests/integration/rule-relevance.test.ts
Test: Rule with breakevenTrigger at 1R is relevant when MFE >= 1R
Test: Rule with breakevenTrigger at 1R is NOT relevant when MFE < 1R
Test: Risk rules (maxRiskPerTrade, etc.) are always relevant
Test: Trade with no MFE data still returns risk rules as relevant
All tests pass (bun run test)
Typecheck passes (bun run check)
userStories.notes: Created tests/integration/rule-relevance.test.ts with 13 tests covering: breakeven trigger relevance above/below/at threshold, risk rules always relevant (maxRiskPerTrade, minRRRatio, dailyLossLimit, maxConcurrentPositions), mixed rules with no MFE data, scaleOutAtR relevance, manual rules always relevant, and trailingStopTrigger relevance.

userStories.title: Remove Badges from RuleChecklist Component
userStories.description: As a user, I want the trade checklist to not show AUTO/SEMI/MANUAL badges so that I see a cleaner interface.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove RuleTypeBadge component definition from src/components/strategy/rule-checklist.tsx
Remove RuleTypeBadge usage from rule rendering
Remove unused imports: Zap, Info, RotateCcw from lucide-react if no longer needed
Typecheck passes (bun run check)
Build passes (bun run build)
Verify in browser: Trade checklist no longer shows badges
userStories.notes: Removed RuleTypeBadge component definition, usage, and unused imports (Zap, Info, RotateCcw). Also removed hasUserOverride variable that was only used for the badge.

userStories.title: Filter Irrelevant Rules in RuleChecklist
userStories.description: As a user, I want the trade checklist to only show rules that were relevant to my trade so that I focus on what matters.
acceptanceCriteria: RuleChecklist component receives relevantRuleIds from query response
Filter rules before rendering to only include those in relevantRuleIds
Rules without autoCondition (pure manual) are always shown
Typecheck passes (bun run check)
Build passes (bun run build)
Verify in browser: Rules that weren't triggered don't appear
userStories.notes: Added relevantRuleIds prop to RuleChecklist, filtering rules via useMemo. Compliance calculation updated to use filtered rules. Backend already includes manual rules in relevantRuleIds.

userStories.title: Remove Badges from StrategyRulesDisplay Component
userStories.description: As a user, I want the strategy rules display to not show AUTO/SEMI/MANUAL badges.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove RULE_TYPE_CONFIG constant from src/components/strategy/strategy-rules-display.tsx
Remove RuleTypeBadge component
Remove badge rendering from rules display
Remove unused imports: Zap, Info from lucide-react if no longer needed
Typecheck passes (bun run check)
Build passes (bun run build)
Verify in browser: Strategy display no longer shows badges
userStories.notes: Removed RULE_TYPE_CONFIG, RuleTypeBadge component, and unused imports (Zap, Info). Also cleaned up Rule interface by removing ruleType field.
